grounding_quotes: <!-- PAGE: 1 -->  GPS Solutions (2023) 27:110 https://doi.org/10.1007/s10291-023-01455-z ORIGINAL ARTICLE An improved BDS‑3 PPP‑B2b positioning approach by estimating signal in space range errors Xiaofei Xu1 · Zhixi Nie1 · Zhenjie Wang1 · Yuanfan Zhang1 · Lin Dong2 Received: 7 November 2022 / Accepted: 7 April 2023 / Published online: 20 April 2023 © The Author(s), under exclusive licence to Sprin
This featured service of BDS- 3, named PPP-B2b service, has captured increasing attention in recent years.

Limited by the distribution and number of permanent monitoring stations, the quality of PPP-B2b orbit and clock correction products is not as good as the products from the real-time service (RTS) of the international GNSS service (IGS).

Unlike IGS-RTS, BDS-3 (IGS) has been providing an open-access real-time PPP PPP-B2b service provides state space representation (SSR) corrections, including orbit correction, clock correction and * differential code bias (DCB) correction, to users through Zhixi Nie niezhixi@upc.edu.cn Geostationary Earth Orbit (GEO) satellite communication (Nie et al.
Typically, these precise rections, including orbit corrections, clock corrections and products are estimated by employing dozens of globally DCB corrections, are currently broadcast to users.
In the proposed approach, ionosphere-free code measurements and ionosphere-free phase measurements are adopted.

reproduction_blockers: No source code or software release
